entry.title: B2B Integration Factory's Role and Value Adds in the B2B Cloud Integration journey
entry.content:
Introduction

Following on the B2B Integration Factory - Custom Extensions blog post, I would like to demonstrate how the B2B Integration Factory packages differ from the standard Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management V2 bundle and how we operate from the backoffice. Further, I also want to emphasize how our B2B Integration Factory service benefit our customers. Lastly, I want to show some features that are already supported by the B2B Integration Factory. 

B2B Integration Factory vs Standard TPM bundle

To summarize it in one sentence: 
B2B Integration Factory cover requirements and provide possible extensions and custom features that are not covered by the Standard yet. Following image shows how we operate and what we envision:

1. Customer asks for a feature request to B2B Integration Factory. It is either possible via SAP Business Accelerator Hub or via mail to b2bintegrationfactory@sap.com
2. B2B Integration Factory collects and prioritizes the requested features. We also analyze, if this feature request can be realized by integration flow extensions. If not, the B2B Integration Factory refers the feature request to the product development (including priority) with the ask for an estimation and delivery date. 
3. Define the details for the feature required and maybe define required custom parameters, which will be used in TPM for the configuration.
4. B2B Integration Factory shows the details of the feature extension to the product development and negotiate on the concepts and custom parameters so that the migration path of the configured data that are required for the feature request are minimal. This will be a contract between the B2B Integration Factory and the product development.
5. Based on the agreements, the B2B Integration Factory informs costumer on the intended concept, when the Factory extension + the standard feature will be delivered.
6. B2B Integration Factory implements the extension into the corresponding B2B Integration Factory packages according the agreements with the product development.
7. B2B Integration Factory publishes a new version of the corresponding B2B Integration Factory packages with new feature at the SAP Business Accelerator Hub à Community Section.
8. B2B Integration Factory informs the customer that new version of the B2B Integration Factory packages with the requested feature is available.
9. Customer downloads the available integration package from the standard or community section accordingly.
10. Product development implements the agreed feature extension into the TPM/IA solution and updates the standard integration package accordingly.
11. B2B Integration Factory adjusts and optimizes the impacted Factory integration flow based on the standard extensions.
12. B2B Integration Factory substitutes the complete corresponding integration flow, if all feature extensions are supported by the standard TPM/IA solution and their corresponding standard integration flow.
13. Product development publishes the updated standard integration package in the Business Accelerator Hub standard section.
14. Customer may substitute the B2B Integration Factory package (provided in the community section) with the standard integration package (provided in the standard section). This can be possible, if all the customer requirements are fulfilled by the standard integration package.

B2B Integration Factory - What are the benefits?

The key assets and value adds of the B2B Integration Factory initiative are:

  1. Fulfilling customer specific B2B requirements at TPM Flow level on demand and in time
  2. Reducing time to delivery and ensuring accelerated delivery in B2B migrations/implementations by using scalable generic packages, which can be used across all customers
  3. Close linkage to the product development and opportunity to influence and shape the product based on new demands from the customers in time
  4. Operational excellence by using B2B Monitoring which helps to monitor the processing interchanges with the different trading partners and is a crucial point for the company's business
  5. Staying up to date with new product features being released 

B2B Integration Factory - Some already available extensions

Looking at the B2B Integration Roadmap, the red marked features are already supported by the B2B Integration Factory:

Teaser

Additionally, we are also developing further extensions requested by customers.
Just to name one extension, it is the so called 'Parking of Messages'. 
Let's say the SAP ERP system is down over the weekend. All messages sent to the SAP ERP system should be parked in order to minimize traffic. Once the SAP ERP system is up and running again, the messages should be processed. 

Please drop a mail and tell us about the particular B2B requirements that are vital to your business.

entry.title: Pivoting to S/4HANA: A Comprehensive Guide for Veteran ABAP Developers
entry.content:
Understand the Basics of S/4HANA 

To fully embrace S/4HANA, you first need to grasp its fundamentals. Unveiled in 2015, S/4HANA leverages the power of the HANA database - SAP's in-memory database solution. With real-time analytics and applications, S/4HANA provides businesses with unprecedented performance and agility. 

SAP S/4HANA, or SAP Business Suite 4 SAP HANA, is SAP's next-generation business suite and the company's biggest innovation since SAP R/3. It is a new product line completely built on the advanced in-memory platform, SAP HANA, and offers a personalized user experience with SAP Fiori.

Here are the basic elements you, as a veteran ABAP Developer, need to understand about S/4HANA:

1. Simplified Data Model: S/4HANA simplifies the data model significantly. It eliminates redundancy, reducing the data footprint and improving transactional speed. Traditional aggregate and index tables have been removed which means the same set of data is no longer updated in multiple tables.

2. Advanced Processing: Utilising the processing power of the HANA in-memory database, S/4HANA enables advanced processing to handle massive volumes of data at high speed. This includes functionalities such as predictive analytics, spatial processing, text analytics, and data virtualization.

3. Real-time Analytics: Unlike older versions of SAP ERP, S/4HANA allows performing real-time analytics at the level of a single transaction. It can run OLTP (Online Transaction Processing) and OLAP (Online Analytical Processing) on a single system.

4. SAP Fiori UX: S/4HANA uses the SAP Fiori user interface, presenting a simplified and intuitive user experience across various devices and deployment options. It significantly enhances user productivity and satisfaction.

5. Scope and Versatility: S/4HANA can be utilized on-premise, in the cloud, or through hybrid models, offering unprecedented versatility. It spans the entire enterprise, encompassing finance, logistics, sales, and business planning among others.

6. Coding Shift: With S/4HANA, you’ll experience a shift in coding since the applications are predominantly written in ABAP but designed to take advantage of HANA's features such as parallelization and columnar storage.

7. HANA-Optimised ABAP: ABAP stays as the main language for S/4HANA, but with new functionalities optimised for SAP HANA database. This includes Database Procedure Proxy, ABAP Managed Database Procedures (AMDP), and Core Data Services (CDS).

Transitioning to S/4HANA calls for a paradigm shift in the way you approach ABAP programming. However, with its simplified architecture and advanced capabilities, learning and adapting to S/4HANA paves the way for great opportunities ahead.

 

Dive into HANA Database 

The backbone of S/4HANA is the HANA database. As an ABAP developer, understanding the internal mechanism of HANA database can provide you a significant edge. The HANA database is column-oriented and in-memory, a big shift from traditional databases. Spend time learning about its unique features like data compression, parallel processing, and built-in analytics.

Understanding the nuances of the HANA database is pivotal for ABAP developers making the transition to SAP S/4HANA. Here's how you can dive into the HANA database.

1. Basics of HANA Database: Understand that SAP HANA is a column-oriented, in-memory database designed for high-performance transactions and real-time analytics. It utilizes in-memory storage to reduce the response times for complex queries and allows multi-threaded, parallel processing for high-speed computations.

2. Study the Architecture: SAP HANA's architecture is an integral part that leverages a multitude of technologies like data virtualisation, advanced analytics, databases and application development. Study HANA's multiple engine types, including Row stores, Column Stores, Object Store, and other service components.

3. SQL and SQLScript: HANA database is primarily managed using SQL and SQLScript. While SQL is used for requesting data, SQLScript is SAP's proprietary, procedural extension to SQL that's used for complex and calculation-heavy operations.

4. Data Modeling and Views: Learn about attribute views, analytic views, and calculation views. Understanding these data models will help you make the best use of the HANA database's real-time computing capabilities.

5. Understand Direct Database Access: With the shift from a code-pushdown paradigm to bringing the code to where data resides, you need to understand how to directly access the database and perform operations in the database layer using ABAP Managed Database Procedures (AMDP).

6. Utilize Training Resources: SAP and other online platforms offer excellent resources for learning HANA, including beginner-friendly tutorials, advanced training, and certification courses.

7. Get Hands-on Experience: Nothing beats practical experience. Use a sandbox system if possible, or consider SAP's HANA Express Edition, which is a streamlined version of the HANA platform that can run on a personal computer.

8. Join SAP Community: Be part of online forums and communities. They provide a platform to discuss doubts, stay updated with the latest changes and interact with other HANA enthusiasts or experts.

Remember, diving into HANA database does not mean you need to forget everything you learned as an ABAP developer, instead, it is about extending your existing ABAP skills into a whole new paradigm. With persistence and active learning, you will find that the transition opens up a new world of possibilities in the SAP space.

 

Learn AMDP (ABAP Managed Database Procedures) 

As you delve into S/4HANA, you’ll encounter AMDP, a critical tool that helps developers manage and control database procedures. AMDP lets you leverage the processing power of HANA by bringing code down to the database layer.

ABAP Managed Database Procedures (AMDP) is a new framework in the AS ABAP system, introduced to allow ABAP developers to write database procedures directly within ABAP. In essence, it represents a paradigm shift, blending the power of ABAP with HANA's advanced database capabilities.

Here's what a seasoned ABAP Developer needs to know about AMDPs:

  1. Procedure Definition: AMDP is essentially a class method defined in ABAP, but implemented in SQLScript - the database manipulation language used in SAP HANA.

  2. Multilingual Intent: AMDP allows combining the logic of ABAP and SQLScript within a single method, enabling ABAP developers to leverage SAP HANA's advanced capabilities directly, without requiring extensive knowledge of SQLScript.

  3. Database Layer Processing: By utilising AMDP, ABAP developers can push computation-intensive operations down to the database layer. This helps leverage SAP HANA’s in-memory processing power for optimal performance.

  4. Transactional Behaviour: AMDPs have integrated transactional behaviour. An AMDP method is executed in the same database session and under the same transaction context as the calling ABAP program.

  5. SQLScript Execution: AMDPs serve as a wrapper around SQLScript code and are executed natively on SAP HANA. This means calculations are performed at the database level, resulting in faster data processing and decreased memory usage in the application server.

  6. Underlying Metadata Access: AMDPs provide access to HANA-specific features such as temporary tables, parallel processing, and partitioning of tables which are unavailable in Open SQL.

Remember, AMDPs don't replace standard ABAP or Open SQL, but rather offer an alternative way to interact with the HANA database. They provide an effective way to bridge the gap between ABAP and SAP HANA, allowing developers to take their data manipulation skills to the next level while making the most of HANA's high-performance capabilities.

 

Spotlight on CDS (Core Data Services) Views 

CDS Views are central to S/4HANA and a departure from classic SE11 views. As an ABAP developer, mastering CDS Views is crucial as they provide a seamless way to define semantic data models and the SQL-based data access layer for any application.

Core Data Services (CDS) Views are a significant development in the SAP HANA database, enabling developers to create semantically rich data models to structure the underlying database layer neatly. Here's what you, as a long-time ABAP developer, need to know about them:

1. SQL-Extended Views: CDS views are extensions of the ubiquitous SQL standard. They encapsulate complex SQL queries into reusable views, dramatically simplifying data access and manipulation.

2. Enhanced Data Modelling: Rather than simply acting as windows onto your tables, CDS views are part of a sophisticated data modelling infrastructure that includes features like associations and path expressions. This leads to cleaner, more intuitive data models.

3. Semantic Layer: CDS views provide a semantic layer (metadata and annotations), resulting in a more meaningful representation of data models. This becomes highly beneficial when you deal with business applications requiring specific data interpretations.

4. Data Services and Consumables: CDS views can expose data services for UIs, enable integration with OData services, and be consumed by analytical tools.

5. Built-In Functions: CDS views come with a set of SQL functions, enabling you to perform complex calculations and operations within the database itself rather than passing data back and forth between your application and the database.

6. ABAP Integration: CDS views are integrated into ABAP Dictionary and can be directly used in ABAP programs using standard Open SQL.

7. Authorisation Management: CDS views support object-based and field-based authorisations, offering flexible and robust security options for data access.

Remember, adopting CDS Views does not mean you will abandon your foundational ABAP knowledge. Instead, think of it as an addition to your current skill set that will help you develop more efficient and powerful applications in the S/4HANA landscape.

 

Explore Fiori and UI5 

User experience upgrades are a major aspect of S/4HANA. SAP's proprietary design language, Fiori, and its client-side HTML5 rendering library, UI5, form the foundation of S/4HANA's user interface. Although not directly in the ABAP domain, understanding and being able to customise the new user interface is a valuable skill-set.

SAP Fiori and SAPUI5 are integral components of the user experience design for modern SAP applications. Here's how understanding these aspects can influence you as veteran ABAP Developer:

SAP Fiori: It is a design language and user experience approach developed by SAP for all their applications. Fiori aims to provide a seamless, intuitive user experience across various platforms and devices. It includes a set of design principles and offers a collection of apps with a simple and easy-to-use experience for broadly used SAP software functions that work seamlessly across devices - desktop, tablet, or smartphone.

SAPUI5: SAPUI5 (or SAP user interface for HTML5), on the other hand, is a collection of libraries for web developers to build responsive web applications, used typically for creating Fiori applications.

Impact on ABAP Developers:

1. New Responsibility Spectrum: The advent of Fiori and SAPUI5 does not replace ABAP but extends your responsibility spectrum to front-end and user-experience design too. While you might be heavily rooted in backend development, understanding these front-end concepts can elevate your development work to align with modern UX practices.

2. Shift in Development Approach: Fiori and SAPUI5 demand a shift from a traditional programming approach to embrace a more design-thinking methodology. You need to consider visual appeal, end-user needs, responsiveness, and adaptability of apps on various platforms.

3. Understanding Fiori Architecture: The Fiori architecture comprises the Fiori Launchpad, Fiori client, and ABAP front-end and back-end servers. Understanding this helps you structure and orient your ABAP code to support Fiori applications.

4. Use of Fiori Elements: Fiori Elements provide a framework to create SAP Fiori applications, reducing the amount of front-end coding required and accelerating application development. Your focus would remain on the back-end services and annotations.

5. Impact on Existing SAP Modules: Fiori is a standard part of S/4HANA, and many traditional SAP GUI transactions now have a Fiori counterpart. Over time, a good grasp of Fiori will be essential in maintaining these pieces of functionality.

6. Skills Upscaling: Learning Fiori and SAPUI5 not only helps you remain up-to-date with industry trends but also can provide an edge in your career, making you a comprehensive ABAP developer with both backend and frontend prowess.

In a nutshell, Fiori and SAPUI5 highlight the importance of user experience and interfaces in modern application development. As an ABAP developer, you're invited to be part of this evolution, creating more user-centered, responsive, and visually appealing SAP solutions.

 

Understand S/4HANA’s New Data Model 

S/4HANA has brought in a simplified data model. Traditional aggregate and index tables (like BSEG and BSIS) were eliminated in favour of more efficient real-time calculations. To make the most of these changes, you'll need to adapt your ABAP programming to S/4HANA’s new data model.

SAP S/4HANA's new data model is one of its most pivotal advancements. Here's a high-level perspective on what the new data model means for you as an experienced ABAP developer:

1. Simplification: The guiding principle of the S/4HANA data model is simplification. Many aggregate and index tables used in previous versions of SAP ERP (like Material Ledger or Profitability Analysis tables) have been eliminated. These are replaced by HANA's in-memory capabilities to perform calculations in real time for analytical purposes.

2. Real-Time Processing: The new data model, leveraged by HANA's columnar in-memory database, allows real-time operational analytics directly on the live transactional data instead of depending on aggregate tables or separate analytical systems.

3. Compatibility Views: To ensure compatibility with previous versions, SAP introduced Compatibility views. It means existing ABAP code can still access, for example, the MKPF and MSEG tables - even though they do not physically exist anymore in S/4HANA. This is achieved using view structures that simulate the old tables.

4. New Tables: New tables have been introduced, such as ACDOCA for Universal Journal, MATDOC for Inventory Management, and PRCD_ELEMENTS for Condition Pricing. These tables store data in a more streamlined manner, reducing redundancy.

5. Impact on ABAP Programs: The new data model impacts traditional ABAP programming in several ways. For instance, standard database operations performed on certain tables like BSEG, KONV, etc., in ECC are now replaced by accessing new tables like ACDOCA and PRCD_ELEMENTS in S/4HANA.

6. CDS Views: Core Data Services (CDS) views are an integral part of the new data model, allowing developers to define semantic, reusable data models in the database layer.

7. Embedded Analytics: The simplified data model in conjunction with the power of HANA's in-memory database enables embedded analytics – allowing analytical operations and transactions to be performed in the same system.

The new data model in S/4HANA indicates a shift from traditional database operations to highly optimised operations suited for an in-memory database like HANA. As an ABAP developer, familiarising yourself with this new data model is essential as it is central to the way SAP S/4HANA operates, influencing how future applications will be developed.

 

Upgrade Your Skills with ABAP for HANA 

ABAP on its own isn’t going away, but it does have new capabilities when used in conjunction with HANA. Acquaint yourself with the concepts of ABAP for HANA, which represent an evolution of the ABAP language toward more integration with the in-memory capabilities offered by the HANA database.

ABAP for HANA integrates the power of HANA within ABAP, allowing the development of highly optimised applications for the SAP HANA database. It's not a new language, but an evolved form of ABAP, wherein existing ABAP skills can be utilised to leverage HANA's capabilities.

Here's how you can broaden your skill set for ABAP for HANA:

1. Understand HANA and Its Advantages: Start by comprehending the fundamentals of SAP HANA and its benefits like in-memory computing and columnar storage.

2. SQL and SQLScript: Upgrade your SQL skills, specifically towards SQLScript, the set of SQL extensions optimized for SAP HANA. Understanding features like the parallel processing of commands and the analysis possibilities for data-intensive calculations at the database level can be handy.

3. Code Pushdown Concept: Learn about the code pushdown concept, which is all about moving data-intensive calculations from the application server to the database layer. This optimizes performance by minimizing data transfer and exploiting the computational capabilities of HANA.

4. Core Data Services (CDS): CDS views enhance the capabilities of ordinary database views by enabling you to define complex views which were not possible earlier. Learning how to use CDS views is fundamental to ABAP for HANA development.

5. ABAP Managed Database Procedures (AMDP): AMDP enables the creation of stored procedures directly in ABAP, where you can put real processing load on the HANA database. This can deliver significant performance improvements for your applications.

6. ALV with Integrated Data Access (ALV with IDA): ALV with IDA allows you to display large amounts of data in ALV format using the computing power of SAP HANA. Familiarize yourself with this to exploit SAP HANA's capabilities more effectively.

7. Utilize Training Resources: SAP offers numerous resources and courses on ABAP for SAP HANA. Explore these to extend your knowledge. Also, consider obtaining a relevant certification which likely enhances your credibility.

8. Experiment in a Sandbox Environment: Get hands-on experience. Apply what you learn in a test or sandbox environment. Code review and testing will give you better insight and understanding.

As you dive into ABAP for HANA, remember that it's more about adding new tools and techniques to your repertoire, rather than discarding your existing ABAP knowledge. Embrace the opportunity to learn and grow with the new advancements in SAP technology. By doing so, you'll position yourself well for the future landscape of the SAP ecosystem.

Extensibility in S/4HANA - Keep that Core Pure

SAP S/4HANA extends the capabilities of traditional ERP systems, combining real-time data, intelligent technologies, and an in-memory database. The extensibility approaches for SAP S/4HANA focus on enhancing the business functionalities according to specific needs without disrupting the core standard system.

There are two key extensibility options that ABAP developers should focus on:

In-App Developer Extensibility: This type of extensibility occurs directly within the S/4HANA system. The nature of the changes can range from simple field adaptations to business logic or UI adjustments. The approach primarily involves ABAP Development Tools in Eclipse (ADT) and the main programming model is called ABAP Programming Model for SAP Fiori. This involves business-oriented development of SAP Fiori applications in ABAP. Developers familiar with ABAP can use Core Data Services (CDS), Business Object Processing Framework (BOPF), SADL, and SAP Fiori Elements.

Side-by-Side Extensibility: This is another real-time integration of native cloud applications developed in SAP Cloud Platform using the available services, and incorporating them into the S/4HANA system processes without the risk of modifying the system itself. By utilising open-standard technologies like HTML5, Java, SAP HANA, and XSJS, developers can create tiny, decoupled applications that handle specific business tasks and connect to S/4HANA through APIs.

Extensions can be created in S/4HANA on-premise and S/4HANA cloud, but there are certain restrictions on the cloud version mainly in order to safeguard best performance, maintenance schedules, and to maintain a clean core. It is suggested to use the cloud platform to augment and complement cloud functionality.

Deciding which approach to implement depends on the specific use case, complexity of the requirement, the nature of the business process to be extended, and how deep in the system the extension should reach.

Traditional ABAP developers will find the transition to these extensibility options smoother if they are comfortable with new tools like Eclipse, modern design principles and web development languages. To stay updated, continuous learning and adoption of new skills is essential for ABAP developers in this technology shift.

Knowledge of OData Services, SAP Gateway, SAP UI5, Fiori, and cloud platform services will be instrumental to make extensions in SAP S/4HANA.

SAP Trainings and Certifications 

Don’t overlook the value of formal training in this journey. SAP offers a myriad of courses tailored to enhance your understanding of S/4HANA. Additionally, obtain an HANA certification to validate your skills, which is also a testament to your competency to prospective employers.

Transitioning to S/4HANA from a traditional ABAP background can be streamlined with the right training and certifications. Here are some of the most relevant ones to assist you in this transformation:

1. Trainings:

  • SAP Learning Hub: SAP Learning Hub provides a vast array of courses over various SAP topics, including S/4HANA and ABAP programming for HANA.

 

  • Introduction to Application Programming on SAP S/4HANA (S4DEV): 
    • This course provides participants with an overview of the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP). The goal is to create an SAP Fiori app touching every area needed to develop and deliver this app to the SAP Fiori launchpad.
    • After a short introduction to SAP S/4HANA and SAP Fiori, the first step is to create CDS projection views on top of existing data model views using the ABAP Development Tools (ADT). Exposing the CDS projection views as a business service makes it possible to consume the model data in a SAPUI5 app, which is built using SAP Fiori elements.
    • Using the behavior of a RAP business object, transactional processing is enabled in the app. Determinations, validations and actions are also added through this. As additional functionality draft-support and value helps are implemented. To make the app available in the SAP Fiori launchpad, catalogs with tiles and target mappings are created.

  • SAP HANA - Programming (HA400):

    • This course is designed for developers and consultants with ABAP programming experience who are transitioning to S/4HANA. 

    • In this course, you learn how you can use SAP HANA and new features in the ABAP programming language to optimize existing ABAP code and to develop innovative ABAP applications to enhance your business.
  • SAP HANA 2.0 SPS06 Modeling (HA300): 
    • This course is also available in a self-paced e-learning format with an active subscription to the SAP Learning Hub, as HA300E.
    • This course teaches the core capabilities of SAP HANA for calculation view modeling, and covers mainly graphical modeling, as well as SQL- based modeling (table functions and procedures) with a focus on performance. It also introduces core data services as a flexible method to model a persistence layer.
    • The participants will gain knowledge about the management of modeling content in the SAP Web IDE for SAP HANA, and the key principles of object and data access security in a modeling context.

  • Extensibility for S/4HANA (S4D425):

    • This course covers the different extensibility approaches for SAP S/4HANA that are available to customers and partners:
      • In-app extensibility focuses on adaptations created by key users that have a deep business knowledge but no technical expertise. In-app extensibility comprises adding new fields or custom logic and adapting the standard UIs. Creating Custom UIs, and custom business objects is also supported. In contrast to in-app extensibility, side-by-side extensibility on SAP Cloud Platform allows developers to build their own applications, enhancing and supplementing SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud.
    • In on-premise environments with SAP S/4HANA, apart from the in-app and side-by-side extensibility one has the full flexibility of extensions developed with ABAP code through the ABAP in Eclipse IDE:
      • This comprises enhancement of CDS views, redefinition of Gateway Services etc.

  • Data Modelling in ABAP Dictionary and ABAP Core Data Services (S4D430) : This course extends the skills that you acquired in the Basic and Intermediate ABAP programming courses with the ability to analyze and define data models. You will define database tables in the ABAP dictionary, and views in ABAP core data services (CDS). You will model relations, add SQL logic, and enrich your views with metadata. In the end, you will protect your data against unauthorized read access and learn how to extend foreign data models.

 

  • Transactional Apps with the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (S4D437) This course covers the various aspect of developing transactional apps with the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP): 
    • Defining the data model and the behavior of a RAP Business Object (BO) with ABAP Core Data Services (ABAP CDS)
    • Using the Entity Manipulation Language (EML) to access RAP BOs
    • Implementing the behavior of a RAP BO with a generic data access layer (managed approach) or based on existing (legacy) code (unmanaged approach).
    • Draft-enabling RAP-based applications
    • Developing SAP Fiori apps based on composite RAP BOs

2. Certifications:

  • SAP Certified Development Specialist - ABAP for SAP HANA 2.0 (E_HANAAW_18) : 
    • The "SAP Certified Development Specialist - ABAP for SAP HANA 2.0" certification exam verifies that the candidate has the knowledge of programming ABAP for SAP HANA required by the profile of an SAP ABAP development consultant. This certificate builds on both the basic knowledge gained in related ABAP-for-HANA training and documentation, as well as on practical experience gained as a member of a SAP HANA team, where the candidate would apply this knowledge practically in projects. A strong background in ABAP programming is highly recommended.

  • SAP Certified Associate – Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud (C_ABAPD_2309) : 

    • The "SAP Certified Associate – Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud" certification exam validates that you possess ABAP programming language fundamentals based on ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model on SAP Business Technology Platform and principles of building custom extensions in SAP S/4HANA Cloud required of the back-end developer profile. This certification proves that you have an overall understanding and in‐depth skills to participate as a member of project team in a mentored role.

  •  SAP Certified Associate – SAP Fiori Application Developer (C_FIORD_2404) : 

    • This certification verifies that you possess the required skills in the area of SAP Fiori application development as it pertains to the Frontend Developer profile. This certification builds on the basic knowledge gained by a developer from formal SAP LEarning training and preferably refined by practical experience within a SAP Fiori application development project team in a mentored role. This is an entry level certification.

Explore these resources, commit to continuous learning, and progressively upgrade your skills. Also consider being part of the SAP community and relevant forums as it allows networking with peers, sharing knowledge, and staying updated on the latest trends and changes in the SAP landscape. Remember that your journey to mastery in S/4HANA as a veteran ABAP Developer involves not just understanding the new system, but also how your role and the nature of your work evolves along with it.

Making a shift from ABAP to S/4HANA is an exciting journey that presents an opportunity to acquire new skills and advance your SAP career. Yes, it can be challenging, but with right mindset, continuous learning and remaining committed to the process, you can smoothly ride the wave of this technology transformation.

entry.title: Purchase Contract Workflow Custom Development Part - 2.
entry.content:
While the standard workflow template [WS20000079] can be used for many scenarios, there may be specific requirements that necessitate custom development. For a comprehensive guide on building custom workflows,  please refer to the provided link. 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/purchase-contract-workflow-custom-development/ba-p/13761664

 Let's now examine some of the challenges encountered during the development of the purchase contract workflow release strategy.

1) Unexpected Blank Purchase Contract Details: -

After creating a purchase contract and checking the My Inbox Fiori app, you might notice that the work item carrying the purchase contract details is blank.

 

 

Cause of the Issue:

Upon inspection, you'll likely discover a "duplication resource error". This error arises because an association within the CDS, C_contractFs --> I_PurchaseContract --> _PurchaseContractItem.PurchasingContractDeletionCode, is being invoked unintentionally. This association retrieves all line items  associated with the purchase contract instead of just the header one. Consequently, multiple line items appear in the header instead of the expected single entry.

Solution:

SAP Note 3391535 [ Manage Purchase Contracts: Duplicate purchase contracts are displayed] addresses this issue. The solution involves removing the association field _PurchaseContractItem.PurchasingContractDeletionCode. This prevents the retrieval of unnecessary data and ensures that only the header purchase contract detail is being displayed in the My Inbox app.

2) Purchase Contract Rejection Issue:

However, even after implementing the BAdI, purchase contract rejection failed. This occurs because the standard implementation utilizes class CL_PO_HEADER_HANDLE_MM (purchase order handler class) for both approving and rejecting purchase contracts. While this class functions as expected for approvals, it doesn't handle rejections correctly. Even after rejection, the purchase contract remains open in the specific release code where it was rejected.

Proposed Solution:

SAP provides a dedicated handler class for purchase contracts, CL_CTR_HANDLER_MM. This class offers a method called set_rejected that performs the exact actions needed for rejection:

  1. Opening the document in change mode
  2. Checking the document
  3. Retrieving header data
  4. Setting the process status to "Release Rejected"

Therefore, to resolve the rejection issue, we can leverage the set_rejected method within class CL_CTR_HANDLER_MM for purchase contract rejections within the BAdI implementation.

3) Approver Comment Retrieval in Release Strategy Workflow

In release strategy workflows for purchase contracts, approver comments are essential. However, the default behaviour doesn't automatically carry over comments from previous approvers to subsequent levels. This occurs because separate work items are triggered for each approval level / release code in release strategy. While this approach facilitates proper delegation, it presents a challenge regarding comment inheritance.

Solution:

This section outlines a solution to retrieve and transfer comments to new work items within the workflow:

  1. Retrieving Previous Work Item IDs:
    • Leverage tables SWW_WI2OBJ and SWWWIHEAD to identify the work item IDs associated with earlier release codes for the same purchase contract.
  2. Extracting Approver Comment:
    • Read the container element named "_DECISION_COMMENT" within the previous work item's container.
    • Utilize a field symbol for efficient memory usage.

Technical Note on Container Data: The "_DECISION_COMMENT" element holds a 90-character string with the following structure (offset notation):

  • Offset 52-54: Object type (3 characters)
  • Offset 55-56: Object year (2 characters)
  • Offset 57-68: Object number (12 characters)
  1. Parsing Container Data (Optional):
    • Functions like SOODK can be used to extract the folder ID and object ID based on the offsets mentioned above.
  2. Reading Approver Comment (Optional):
    • The function module "SO_NOTE_READ" can be employed to retrieve the actual approver comment using the extracted folder ID and object ID.
  3. Transferring Comment to New Work Item (Optional):
    • The function module "SAP_WAPI_ATTACHMENT_ADD" allows attaching the retrieved comment to the current work item.
    • Set appropriate parameters:
      • file_type = 'T' (Text)
      • file_extension = 'HTM' (HTML format for readability)

entry.title: Mass enrichment of business partners with Tax and ID numbers found using the CDQ data provider
entry.content:
Abstract

In this blog post you'll find information about the "Reference Data Lookup" step that can be put into process templates for the Manage Governance Processes Business Partners (link to be updated) app. The "Reference Data Lookup" step automatically enriches business partners with Tax and ID numbers found with the help of the third-party data provider CDQ. If the automatic enrichment is not successful, the user can manually follow up with the Look Up Reference Data for Business Partners (link to be updated) app.

 

Prerequisites for using the Reference Data Lookup step as a customer

  • SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (link to be updated) 2023 Feature Pack Stack 02
  • Contract with CDQ AG, see SAP Store
  • Using Cloud-Ready Mode
    Below you can find a basic comparison of cloud-ready mode and classic mode for orientation. See Comparing Cloud-Ready Mode and Classic Mode for more details.

  • Follow the setup Configure Reference Data Lookup Step (link to be updated) 

 

In a nutshell

The image below shows you the result of the Reference Data Lookup step within a consolidation process in the Manage Governance Processes Business Partners (link to be updated) app. In addition to that also mass, single and federated processes are supported. By choosing the "Show Details" button the user sees which BP (business partner) was changed and which number was added - in this case VAT DE342757812.

Overall, the Reference Data Lookup step has the following qualities:

  • Works out-of-the-box
    After the setup, including adding the user and password from CDQ, no business rules need to be added to make the step work
  • Works automatically
    The step is intended to run through without user intervention.
  • Enrich as many business partners as possible while not enriching false-positives
    By applying the strategy of careful matching and enriching, the functions avoids matching to a wrong company and enriching incorrect details.
  • Uses CDQ's golden record
  • Adding only missing Tax and ID numbers while respecting system checks

 

Step Logic

Within the step the following diagram shows in a simplified way, the ideal path (in pink) for processing individual business parners. The actual, more detailed flow would show how that simple flow is different for mass processing (for example, business partners are collected and sent together in batches to avoid too many requests) and the split of responsibility within the process between SAP and the data provider CDQ. 

 

 

In the above image you see that not only business data (second green box), but also the origin of the business data (first green box) is added into the BP as Reference Record Links. Reference Record IDs stored in the BP are called Reference Record Links. Here is an example based on the Look Up Reference Data for Business Partners (link to be updated) app:

There are 2 ways to create such a link – both ensuring the high quality of that information:

  • As a user using the Look Up app to choose the right Reference Record and then creating a link. Manually typing in such a number in the BP is not possible.
  • In the background, during the Reference Data Lookup step the link is created by the system.

The Reference Data Lookup step trusts this link over other business data. If the business partner contains a reference record link, the data provider should return the current data for this linked reference record and not search to find a better match to another reference record.

In the example above (from the very beginning), the "4 ID numbers added across 2 BPs" contain 2 business numbers (one being Commercial Register Number - BUP002, HRB 20552) and 2 Reference Record Links (one being Corporate Data Quality - DPI001, GR.DE.RC:B8536_HRB722007).

 

DEMO

See below a video for different ways to work with the results of the Reference Data Lookup step. Click the image or follow the link Reference Data Lookup step in action.

Author

Martin Steinhauser, Product Owner for Master Data Quality Services focussing on Data Provider Integration topics.

entry.title: How to remove serial number inconsistencies by doing a physical inventory count
entry.content:
Consider: To avoid inconsistencies in the first place the stock check in the serial number profile assigned to your material should be set to "Error" and the serial number usage in the MMSL procedure must be set to "Obligatory" (03).
Setting the stock check to "Error" will cause that a user cannot proceed when about to post inconsistent serial numbers with stock. Setting the MMSL procedure to "Obligatory" will make it mandatory to enter serial numbers with stock postings that create material documents.

Serial number inconsistencies in this context mean that the stock quantity and serial number quantity do not align. That might lead to unexpected stock counts like serial numbers not being available in a location where they are expected or more serial numbers in a location than stock.

In case you were about to do a physical inventory count posting for your serial number inconsistency and encountered error IO232: “Status errors occurred for the serial numbers concerned”, please check that the second prerequisite regarding the statuses is fulfilled and read section 3. It might be that the serial number you're trying to post is already assigned to other stock indicators.

Prerequisites:

  • To do a physical inventory count with serial numbers, the serial number usage in the procedure MMSL (Maintain goods receipt and goods issue document) needs to be set to "Obligatory" (03)
  • To post a goods issue for a serial number in a physical inventory count, it must not be in one of these statuses: assigned in delivery note (EDEL), deletion flag (DLFL), deletion indicator (DLT), object deactivated (INAC) or ref. object under construction (UNCO)

The following steps describe how to remove a serial number inconsistency:

  1. Use the “Display Serialized Stock Quantities” app (F7551) to find out if there are any inconsistencies between your stock and the serial numbers for a certain plant.

    In this example there is one inconsistency in storage location 101A, because there is one stock quantity in blocked stock and one serial number without stock in unrestricted-use stock.

  2. To resolve such an inconsistency, do a physical inventory count in your storage location.
    Navigate to the app “Manage Physical Inventory Count” (F5430) and press the button “Ad Hoc Count”.

    Create an ad hoc count for the storage location with the inconsistency.


    After doing the physical inventory count for blocked and unrestricted-use stock, in this example, we notice that the stock and the serial number are in blocked stock.
    The counted stock quantities are entered for the two items in the draft document of the physical inventory count which has one item for blocked stock with counted quantity 1 PC and one item for unrestricted-use stock with counted quantity 0 PC.


    Using the chevron, navigate to the object page of the item with stock to enter the corresponding serial number.

    In the "Serial Numbers" tab enter the serial number which is in unrestricted-use stock.

    “Apply” and “Create” the count document when you are finished with your count.

  3. The serial number inconsistency in this example is caused by the serial number having the wrong stock identifiers (stock type unrestricted-use stock instead of blocked stock). Since serial numbers can only have one assignment of stock identifiers (location, stock type, batch, etc.), we first need to remove the current stock identifiers from our serial number.
    So the physical inventory count in this case needs to be posted in a two-step procedure.
    1. First post the removal of the serial number(s) in this case the counting of the unrestricted-use stock to “free” the serial number(s) for usage.


      Confirm.

    2. Second post the item with stock and serial number(s).

      Confirm.

      If in this case you don't do the two-step procedure and try to post a serial number, which still exists in the system in another location you will encounter error IO232: “Status errors occurred for the serial numbers concerned”.

       

  4. If you check the “Display Serialized Stock Quantities” app again the inconsistency should be gone.

     

We hope you liked this blog and it helps you to remove any serial number inconsistency that might block you. If you would like us to add additional information, please comment below.

entry.title: How to schedule an ILM job for deleting employee data
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP Information Lifecycle Management (SAP ILM) enhances the SAP standard delivery with the ability to manage the lifecycle of live and archived data based on rules. To delete employee data, we need to schedule an SAP Information Lifecycle Management job that periodically archives or deletes terminated employees.

Purpose:

The purpose of this blog is to demonstrate how we can delete terminated employees in the system.

Target Audience:

This blogpost is targeted towards SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementers and consultants, project managers, technical & functional consultants, ILM experts who are looking for a one place information for standard documentation around deletions, blocking, data archiving and data destruction of worker data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Disclaimer: 

This blog is being written today (25th Sep 2024) and is applicable as of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release. Features & capabilities are subjected to change as per SAP's product development roadmap, hence always consider looking at the latest standard documentation.

"Image/data in this KBA is from SAP internal systems, sample data, or demo systems. Any resemblance to real data is purely coincidental."

Prerequisites:

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2408 release, there are two main options for this step. Using the application ILM Audit Area and ILM Policies, or alternatively using the configuration activities.

Option 1 - Using ILM relevant applications: Add necessary business role and catalog to business user by following KBA 3507687 - ILM Audit area, ILM policies, and Manage ILM Object Groups apps are not available in the successor catalog.

Option 2 - Using configuration activities: SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT (business catalog ID: SAP_CA_BC_IC_LND_ILM_PC) role is required to open the relevant configuration activities in SAP Central Business Configuration or in Manage Your Solution app.

 

In this blog, let’s use the option 2 to remove terminated worker data.

Procedure:

There are three sets of data, work assignment, business partner and business user.

To delete employee data, we need to schedule an SAP Information Lifecycle Management job that periodically archives or deletes terminated employees.

For more details, please refer to SAP Help Portal - Data Management for HR Connectivity and Workforce Details

Note

The SAP ILM process archives or deletes all the workforce persons who are terminated in the system. This process cannot be followed for a specific workforce person or a set of workforce persons.

1. Terminate Employment or Work Agreement

Terminate an employment or work agreement in the source system where it was created. To terminate a work agreement using the Manage Workforce app by changing the end date, refer to Manage Workforce.

2. Lock Unused Business Users

You can lock unused business users. For more information, see How to Lock Unused Business Users.

3. Assign Residence and Retention audit area to WFD_ASSIGNMENT ILM object.

Open configuration activity ‘Assign ILM Object To Audit Area’ (ID: 106661)

*If this is the first time to use ILM process, make sure that Audit area is already created by configuration activity ‘Maintain Audit Area’ (ID: 106660). Please see the details on SAP Help Portal - How To Maintain Audit Areas.

Policy Category: RST Residence Rules
Audit Area: ARCHIVING Data Archiving

Click on 'Run'

Select ILM Object: WFD_ASSIGNMENT
Status 'Active'
Click on 'Save'

Policy Category: RTP   Retention Rules
Audit Area: BUPA_DP   Business Partner: EoP Lock

Click on 'Run'

Select ILM Object: WFD_ASSIGNMENT
Status 'Active'
Click on 'Save'

** If there are multiple Audit Areas assigned to one ILM object 'WFD_ASSIGNMENT', system will not be able to identify the correct audit area and the blocking will be failed. Hence always make sure that for one ILM object only one Audit area is assigned.

ILM Audit Areas - SAP Help Portal

4. Create Residence and Retention rules.

Open configuration activity ‘Maintain ILM Policies and Rules’ (ID: 106666)

Policy Category: Residence Rules
Audit Area: ARCHIVING   Data Archiving
ILM Object: WFD_ASSIGNMENT   Destruction Object for Worker Assignment

Click on ‘Run’
Click on ‘New Entries’
Enter Policy Name and click on ‘Save’

Select newly created Policy, double click ‘Condition Fields’, click on ‘New Entries’
Select COUNTRY as Condition Field, and enter Position
Double click ‘Residence Rules’, click on ‘New Entries’

Enter the Residence Period
Select Time Unit
Select Time Reference WFD_ASSGMT_END_DATE

Double click Policies and change the policy status from not live to live and click on ‘Save’

Policy Category: Retention Rules
Audit Area: BUPA_DP   Business Partner: EoP Lock
ILM Object: WFD_ASSIGNMENT   Destruction Object for Worker Assignment

Click on ‘Run’
Click on ‘New Entries’
Enter Policy Name and click on ‘Save’

Select newly created Policy, double click ‘Condition Fields’, click on ‘New Entries’
Select COUNTRY as Condition Field, and enter Position
Double click ‘Retention Rules’, click on ‘New Entries’

Enter Min Retention Period and Max Retention Period
Select Time Unit
Select Time Reference: WFD_ASSGMT_END_DATE

Double click Policies and change the policy status from not live to live and click on ‘Save’

5. Schedule ILM Destruction job in ILM Data Destruction application.

Go to ILM Data Destruction application

Select the Data Destruction Objects and toggle to Not Scheduled.
In the search box type WFD_ASSIGNMENT and select the check box
Click on ‘Schedule’

Use the Test check box for simulating the deletion. (Optional)

Data destruction job will run every Saturday at 22:00 UTC in the production mode and everyday at 4:00 UTC in test mode.

6. Blocking and Deleting Business Users

While user master records can be simply deleted from the system, think of the consequences to any references to that user master record before deleting user data. For more information, see Blocking and Deleting Business Users.

7. Archiving and Destroying Business Partner Data

Use the archiving object CA_BUPA to archive business partner data. For more information, refer to Archiving Business Partner Using CA_BUPA.

 

Note

While deleting/blocking the work agreements, certain veto checks can be found (message like: Blocking not possible because veto check failed for assignment XXXX). In this case, make sure all of them are cleared, then run the process again.

Certain examples of veto checks are employees who need to be deleted are already part of a posted Purchase requisition or involved in the commercial/enterprise project (<Personnel number> is assigned to a responsibility in project <Project ID>).

entry.title: Automate assignment of Users to Teams
entry.content:
Steps to enable this Feature for the Existing SPM Incentive Management and Embedded Analytics Customers

  1. Login as Sales Portal Administration in Incentive Management
  2. Go to Global Settings Page and Enable the Below option under “ Authentication Settings”       

In Database the following parameter will be set to ‘TRUE’ once the above parameter is enabled in GLOBAL_SETTINGS table

3. Now go to your Identity Provisioning Service(IPS) – Target System -   SAC Transformations do the below changes mentioned in BOLD and marked as GREEN

  1. {
  2.     "user": {
  3.         "condition": "($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_COMM-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_CPQ-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_TTQT-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_WKFL-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_COMM-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_WKFL-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_TTQT-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_CPQ-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_COMM-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_WKFL-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_CPQ-SCAN') || ($.groups[?(@.value =~ /COM_SLS_ROLE.*/)].value EMPTY false) || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_TTQT-SCAN')",
  4.         "mappings": [
  5.             {
  6.                 "sourcePath": "$.schemas",
  7.                 "targetPath": "$.schemas",
  8.                 "optional": true,
  9.                 "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
  10.             },
  11.             {
  12.                 "sourcePath": "$.id",
  13.                 "targetVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem"
  14.             },
  15.             {
  16.                 "sourcePath": "$.id",
  17.                 "targetPath": "$.userName"
  18.             },
  19.             {
  20.                 "sourcePath": "$.userName",
  21.                 "targetPath": "$['urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:user-custom-parameters:1.0']['extensionProperty1']"
  22.             },
  23.             {
  24.                 "sourcePath": "$.id",
  25.                 "targetPath": "$.id"
  26.             },

  29.                 "targetPath": "$.name"
  30.             },

  33.                 "targetPath": "$.displayName"
  34.             },

  37.                 "targetPath": "$.active",
  38.                 "optional": true
  39.             },
  40.             {

  43.                 "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
  44.             },
  45.             {

  48.                 "constant": true

  56.             {

  58.                 "condition": "($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_COMM-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_CPQ-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_TTQT-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'ADMINISTRATOR_WKFL-SCAN')",
  59.                 "constant": "PROFILE:SCAN:SCA_Scheduler",
  60.                 "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true

  65.                 "constant": "PROFILE:SCAN:SCA_ContentMigrator",
  66.                 "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
  67.             },
  68.             {

  70.                 "condition": "($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_COMM-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_CPQ-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_TTQT-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHOR_WKFL-SCAN')",
  71.                 "constant": "PROFILE:SCAN:SCA_Author",
  72.                 "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true
  73.             },
  74.             {

  76.                 "condition": "($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_COMM-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_CPQ-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_TTQT-SCAN') || ($.groups[*].value contains 'AUTHENTICATED_WKFL-SCAN')",
  77.                 "constant": "PROFILE:SCAN:SCA_Viewer",

  79.             },

  83.                 "optional": true,

  88.                 ]
  89.             }
  90.         ]
  91.     },
  92.     "group": {
  93.         "ignore": false,
  94.  
  95.         "condition": "(($.id EMPTY false) || ($.displayName EMPTY false)) && ($.displayName contains 'COM_SLS_ROLE')",
  96.         "mappings": [

  101.                 "preserveArrayWithSingleElement": true

  111.                 "functions": [

  114.                         "beginIndex": 13
  115.                     }
  116.                 ]
  117.             },

  119.                 "sourcePath": "$.displayName",
  120.                 "targetPath": "$.displayName",
  121.                 "functions": [

  125.                     }

  127.             },

  142.                     }
  143.                 ]

  145.         ]
  146.     }
  147. }

 4.Create a folder along with team creation with the same name below property should be added in the properties of SAC Target System

5.Please make sure "ignore": false under “group” is present. If it is true, change it to false.

6.In addition if the following entries exist,     

 

 

 

 

 

{

                "sourcePath": "$.displayName",

                "targetPath": "$.id",

                "targetPath": "$.id",

                "sourceVariable": "entityIdTargetSystem"

            },

entry.title: SAP Ariba Shopping App or SAP Ariba Procurement App? What fits best for me?
entry.content:
Hello Ariba Community,

This blog post aims to offer a comprehensive comparison of the functionality and product support provided by the SAP Ariba Shopping app and the SAP Ariba Procurement app, assisting you in determining which app is best suited for your specific procurement needs.

In essence, the SAP Ariba Shopping app, developed on the SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP), is a mobile native application that empowers employees and managers to navigate and shop from company catalogs. It is worth noting that the forthcoming 2411 update will introduce support for Level 1 punchout catalogs.

The shopping experience is comparable with shopping apps for consumers, therefore only minimal training is required to use the app. As shopping should be simple and fun you have certain restrictions on catalog item types and the ability of changing the purchase requisition. For those use cases, we recommend using the SAP Ariba Procurement app which is designed for procurement professionals and employees with advanced shopping needs such as ordering configurable items or doing an account split on item level.

Good news is that you can use both apps in parallel either for one user or for different user groups.

Let's first delve into the commercial conditions. The table below illustrates the Ariba products supported by each app:

 

Product                             

SAP Ariba Shopping

SAP Ariba Procurement

SAP Ariba Buying

supported

supported

SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing

supported

supported

SAP Ariba SNAP

 

supported

SAP Ariba Sourcing

 

supported

SAP Ariba Contracts

 

supported

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance Management

 

supported

If your organization has subscribed any of the listed products, you are entitled to use the app without incurring any additional license costs. However, it is important to be aware of any potential limitations in how the apps are utilizing the supported Ariba products:

 

Product / Product Scope

SAP Ariba Shopping

SAP Ariba Procurement

SAP Ariba Buying

Support for company catalogs

Support for company catalogs

SAP Ariba Buying and Invoicing

Support for company catalogs

Support for company catalogs

SAP Ariba SNAP

 

Support for company catalogs

SAP Ariba Buying with guided buying capability

Support for company catalogs and planned for 2411 punchout L1

Not supported:

  • Intelligent product recommendations
  • Policies
  • Favorites
  • Guided Buying tiles

Support for company catalogs.

Not supported:

  • Intelligent product recommendations
  • Policies
  • Favorites
  • Guided Buying tiles

Integration of guided buying with S/4 HANA or S/4 HANA Cloud (2NV/3EN scenario)

Both apps do not support requisitions when guided buying is integrated with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud (2NV/3EN)

Both apps do not support requisitions created when guided buying is integrated with SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud  (2NV/3EN)

SAP Ariba Sourcing

 

Task management is supported

SAP Ariba Contracts

 

Task management is supported

SAP Ariba Supplier Lifecycle and Performance Management

 

Support for:

  • New supplier creation
  • Supplier request tracking

 

Here is the functionality comparison of shopping for both apps:

 

Shopping Functionality

SAP Ariba Shopping

SAP Ariba Procurement

Shopping goods from company catalogs

supported

supported

Shopping goods from L1 punchout catalogs

support planned for 2411 Ariba release

 

Non catalog request

supported

supported

Mandatory items

supported

supported

Track & trace purchase requisition approvals

supported

supported

Support of BME catalog items

 

supported

Configurable items (parametric/partial items)

 

supported

Edit item price

 

supported

Buy again

 

supported

Add account distributions

 

supported

Shop on behalf of another user

 

supported

Post Goods Receipt

 

supported

Change Purchase order

 

supported

 

Lastly, let's explore the roadmap for both applications. We are currently revising our roadmaps but one direction we can share is that we are thriving to enable Joule integration into our mobile app portfolio. As soon as we have clarity, I will share the roadmap items in a new blog post.

Enjoy shopping!

 

Michael Spiess

SAP Ariba Product Management

 

Further References:

  • New blog post about the latest release V1.12.0
  • Download (iOS/Android) SAP Ariba Shopping app 
  • Watch SAP Ariba Shopping App on Youtube
  • SAP Ariba Shopping roadmap for iOS and Android
  • SAP Ariba Procurement roadmap for iOS and Android
  • Blog post: Getting started with SAP Ariba Shopping App in 7 steps
  • SAP help: Getting started with SAP Ariba Procurement app

entry.title: SAP Fieldglass: Key Configurations and Features You Need to Know
entry.content:
1. Advisors: Advisor reports are a crucial tool that provides users with valuable information to help guide them through the decision-making process while acting on, approving, and managing records and documents within SAP Fieldglass.
  2. Artificial Intelligence: SAP Fieldglass has several features powered by AI and Machine Learning, that enhance the application by optimizing various aspects of Contingent and Services workflows. For example, our Resume Ranking feature uses machine learning to give hiring managers an edge in their hiring process by evaluating how closely a resume matches a job's requirements in comparison to other resumes being considered.
  3. Configuration Manager: Configuration Manager is a separate user role within SAP Fieldglass that gives users access to integration, system, and maintenance tools via a Self-Service Dashboard. The tools allow you to perform many administrative actions independently, eliminating the need to open support cases with SAP Fieldglass to implement those actions in your company.
  4. Quarterly Trends Dashboard: The Quarterly Trends Dashboard highlights key analytics and metrics, enabling better visibility into program performance and quick benchmarking against peers. Users can review performance against 50+ KPIs, filter the data by category, and easily export the results.
  5. Rates 2.0: Rates 2.0 is the evolution of rate management in SAP Fieldglass. It allows users to manage rate structures with less complexity, simplified configuration, and greater efficiency.

You’ll find more information about each of the top five key configurations on the SAP Help Portal:

  • Advisors
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Configuration Manager
  • Quarterly Trends Dashboard
  • Rates 2.0

You can also join the SAP Fieldglass Product Success Work Zone for access to our key configuration guides.

entry.title: Premium Hub CoE - DSC Knowledge Bits - Cookbook for Advanced Shipping and Receiving
entry.content:
Dear TM and EWM friends and fans,

hopefully all of you have heard about Advanced Shipping and Receiving (Adv. SR, sometimes referred to as ASR) by now, which is the new integration between S/4 HANA TM and S/4 HANA EWM. If not, I'd recommend this blog article by @Jörg_Michaelis  for an introduction and this blog article for some more insights. Additionally check out this Learning Journey and then you should have a good understanding of the topic 😉

Now, SAP have released a new Consulting Note 3512117 - Advanced Shipping and Receiving - Cookbook which includes a holistic set-up and customizing guide as well as a ste-by-step guide how to execute best practise scenarios. Hopefully this will help customers, partners and consultants with implementing best practice scenarios. Please note, this is a first version based on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 and going forwards the cookbook will be updated and maintained to stay on track with latest changes and improvements.
